# Extracao: transcription_pdf_M2_DealTeamChecklist

---

There are three steps to putting together your deal team.
Step 1: Identify an M&A Lead Advisor
Step 2: Identify supporting professional advisors
Step 3: DRIVE the deal team to attack speed
Here’s a checklist to make sure you’re remembering everything as you line-up your deal
team…
STEP 1: Lead Advisor:
• 	If you’ve already identified a business, reach out to 10XBuySell and tell them you’re
a 10X Business Blueprint student. Give them a first chance to tell you if our team can
handle advising you on your deal.
• 	If not, start your outreach to advisors using the script sample in the resources
section and other outreach methods.
• 	When you’re interviewing a lead advisor, ask: ““What’s the biggest successful business
acquisition you’ve worked on?”
• 	Has the advisor answered that he’s advised a buyer on as big or bigger than the one
you’re targeting? If the answer is no or a smaller deal than yours, move on.
• 	Does the lead advisor candidate have multiple experiences in M&A? If not, move on.
• 	Share your deal vision with them. Discuss with them how they fit into making your
first acquisition a success and how they'll benefit when you both reach the goal line
Deal Team Checklist
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 1 of 3 --

• 	If they agree, can you pay them? If so, negotiate their fee in line with your deal
vision.
• 	Are you prioritizing little money out of pocket? If so, try to negotiate a contingent
advisor fee but understand this might limit you.
• 	Ask your lead advisor (after they're onboard) if they have recommendations for an
accountant… lawyer… or commercial real estate broker that they've either worked
with before or that they know would be a good fit for your deal vision.
STEP 2: Identify Supporting Professional Advisors:
• 	Find an accountant that’s worked on a deal as big or bigger than the one you’re
targeting.
• 	Find a lawyer that’s worked on a deal as big or bigger than the one you’re targeting.
• 	Share your deal vision with them. Discuss with them how they fit into making your
first acquisition a success and how they'll benefit when you both reach the goal line.
• 	If they agree, can you pay them? If so, negotiate their fee in line with your deal
vision.
• 	Are you prioritizing little money out of pocket? If so, try to negotiate a contingent
advisor fee but understand this might limit you.
STEP 3: DRIVE Your Deal Team to Attack Speed
• 	Meet with your deal team and set the vision for your ideal acquisition and how
everyone on the deal team participates and benefits from that outcome.
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 2 of 3 --

• 	Assign areas of accountability between you, your deal advisor, your lawyer and your
accountant.
• 	Assign milestones and deadlines for the different part of the deal process we’re
teaching you in this course.
• 	Meet & communicate regularly with your deal team about the current status of those
action items and keep up the attack speed.
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 3 of 3 --
